2604.19189.title: Headlines You Won't Forget: Can Pronoun Insertion Increase Memorability?
2604.19189.paper_summary: For news headlines to influence beliefs and drive action, relevant information needs to be retained and retrievable from memory. In this probing study we draw on experiment designs from cognitive psychology to examine how a specific linguistic feature, namely direct address through first- and second-person pronouns, affects memorability and to what extent it is feasible to use large language models for the targeted insertion of such a feature into existing text without changing its core meaning. Across three controlled memorization experiments with a total of 240 participants, yielding 7,680 unique memory judgments, we show that pronoun insertion has mixed effects on memorability. Exploratory analyses indicate that effects differ based on headline topic, how pronouns are inserted and their immediate contexts. Additional data and fine-grained analysis is needed to draw definitive conclusions on these mediating factors. We further show that automatic revisions by LLMs are not always appropriate: Crowdsourced evaluations find many of them to be lacking in content accuracy and emotion retention or resulting in unnatural writing style. We make our collected data available for future work.
2604.19189.paper_summary_zh: 為了讓新聞標題能夠影響信念並驅動行動，相關資訊需要被保留並能夠從記憶中檢索。在這項探討性研究中，我們借鑒了認知心理學的實驗設計，以檢視一個特定的語言特徵，即通過第一人稱和第二人稱代詞的直接稱呼，如何影響記憶力，以及在多大程度上可以使用大型語言模型將這種特徵有針對性地插入現有文本中，而不改變其核心意義。在三個受控的記憶實驗中，共有240名參與者，產生了7,680個獨特的記憶判斷，我們顯示代詞插入對記憶力的影響是混合的。探索性分析表明，這些影響根據標題主題、代詞的插入方式及其即時上下文而有所不同。需要額外的數據和細緻的分析來得出對這些中介因素的明確結論。我們進一步顯示，LLM的自動修訂並不總是合適的：群眾評估發現許多修訂在內容準確性和情感保留方面都存在不足，或導致不自然的寫作風格。我們將收集的數據提供給未來的研究使用。
2604.19189.author: Selina Meyer et.al.
2604.19189.authors: Selina Meyer, Magdalena Abel, Michael Roth
